_note: SAMPLE / DEMO DATA — Pathfinder decision-engine questions. Each option contributes weights (0–5) to the veteran's profile across: hands, tech, people, data, lead, risk, physical, outdoor, care, autonomy. 'speed' options tag pace preference. This is a decision aid, not a clinical instrument. Question design informed by the U.S. Department of Labor's O*NET® Interest Profiler framework (Holland RIASEC) and O*NET Work Values; VetPath is an independent tool — USDOL/ETA has not approved, endorsed, or tested this adaptation.

questions.label: What kind of work pulls at you most?

options.label: Building or fixing things with my hands
Helping or taking care of people

questions.label: Where do you want to spend your workday?

options.label: A mix — variety keeps me sharp

questions.label: How much structure do you want?

options.label: Clear mission, clear SOPs — tell me the standard
Give me the goal, I'll pick the route
Full autonomy — I want to own the whole thing

questions.label: Paycheck vs. bet-on-yourself — where are you?

options.label: Mostly steady, some upside is fine
I'm ready to bet on myself

questions.label: How physical do you want the work to be?

options.label: Very — I want to move all day
Somewhat — on my feet, not breaking my back

questions.label: How much people-contact do you want?
How do you feel about details, records, and keeping things squared away?

options.label: I like it — logs, checklists, standards done right
I can do it, but I'd rather own the bigger picture
Paperwork drains me — keep me off the forms

questions.label: How fast do you need the first real paycheck on this path?

options.label: A few months of training is fine
A full degree is worth it to me

questions.label: Do you want to lead again?

options.label: Eventually, once I've learned the terrain
I'd rather master the craft than manage

questions.label: What kind of place do you want to live?

freePrompt: In your own words: what does a good life after service look like for you? (optional — helps us tailor your plan)
